needle_load.note: Claude includes a 5s idle period before needle_load then 10s of loading (15s combined); GPT-4o starts clock at first instrument and assigns 4s. Combined idle+needle_load Claude=15s vs GPT-4o=4s (delta 11s).

suture_placement.note: Major disagreement — Claude assigns 50s to placement, GPT-4o only 20s. Difference migrates into downstream phases.

second_throw.note: GPT-4o more than doubles Claude's second_throw estimate.

third_throw.note: Largest single phase delta in any comparison so far — GPT-4o assigns 83s to third_throw, Claude only 33s. GPT-4o absorbs the prolonged late-task reinforcing manipulation into throw 3; Claude puts it into the cutting phase.

suture_cut.note: Mirror of the third_throw disagreement — Claude 50s vs GPT-4o 11.7s. Sum of third_throw + suture_cut: Claude 83s, GPT-4o 94.7s (delta 11.7s). Sum of second+third+cut: Claude 105s, GPT-4o 144.7s — still disagreement even after collapsing the boundary.

gap_visible.note: Same pattern as V14 — Claude flags a possible gap (very_low confidence, 'unclear_partial' closure), GPT-4o sees no gap and 'near_complete' closure. This divergence accounts for most of the 4.5pt penalty delta.

placement_alignment.note: Both teachers low/very_low confidence — monitor recording limits exact mm measurement, but the estimates themselves are within 0.5 mm.

fls_score_reconciliation.reconciliation_note: Strongest teacher agreement in the entire V-series — ΔFLS 1.2, even tighter than V14's 1.5. Both teachers converge on total time (within 3.3s) and both reach the same bottom-line judgment: competent completion with major efficiency deficits, no automatic-zero events. The 4.5pt penalty gap is entirely Claude's gap-visible concern, which Claude itself rates very_low confidence. GPT-4o's 398.2 is likely the more reliable point estimate; a 397.6 midpoint is a safe reconciled value.

systematic_findings.boundary_disagreement: The two teachers disagree massively on WHERE the time goes in this video (placement vs throws vs cut) but agree almost exactly on the TOTAL. This is a strong signal that phase-by-phase training signals should be used with caution — only aggregate time and overall penalties are robust across teachers. Recommend training downstream models on total_time + penalty breakdown, not raw phase durations.
systematic_findings.drain_gap_divergence_recurrence: V11 and V14 both show Claude flagging a possible drain gap on monitor-filmed videos while GPT-4o sees complete/near-complete closure. This now looks like a systematic Claude bias on low-resolution monitor recordings, not a real finding. Recommend down-weighting Claude's gap-visible signal on any video with 'phone filming monitor' recording notes until ground truth is established.
systematic_findings.late_task_regrasping_consensus: Despite disagreeing on where it lives (throws vs cut), both teachers identify the late-task segment (from ~105s onward) as the dominant inefficiency. The improvement suggestions converge: shorter working loops, earlier symmetric opposing tension, and earlier cutter exchange prep.
